commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f79e2af72a801b27f778bcf0f1d9d0f3119b4e13)
commands.review_summary:
This PR passes `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` into both CLI and embedded isolated cron executor calls so empty cron assistant output becomes silent success.

PR surface: Source +7. Total +7 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main routes cron CLI and embedded runs without the silent-empty flag, while the CLI runner throws `empty_response` unless that flag is true; I did not run a live cron in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed to choose the cron silent-empty compatibility policy and require current-head proof; this is not a safe automated repair while sibling PRs and the canonical issue remain open.

actions.reason: repair worker already active for this job path

target.title: fix: #94224 ÕģüĶ«Ė cron lane ķØÖķ╗śÕø×ÕżŹ’╝īķü┐ÕģŹń®║Õø×ÕżŹ FailoverError
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94224 ŌĆö Cron jobs that intentionally produce empty assistant replies fail with `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response. (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")`.

The auto-reply path (DM/group chat) supports silent replies via `silentReplyPolicy`. A cron job runs in an isolated lane with no chat context ŌĆö there is no user to "reply" to. An empty assistant response from a condition-check-only cron job is expected behavior (e.g., "check a condition, only notify if it changed"). Yet the cron executor never passed `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`, so every silent tick threw `FailoverError` and appeared as `status: error` in `cron list`.

## Change

`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts` ŌĆö +2 lines, same flag in both runner paths.

| Location | Line | Change |
|----------|------|--------|
| CLI runner params | 321 | `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: !params.deliveryRequested` |
| Embedded agent runner params | 378 | `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: !params.deliveryRequested` |

**Gating on `deliveryRequested`:**

The flag is NOT unconditional. It is gated by whether the cron job requested delivery:

| Scenario | `deliveryRequested` | `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` | Behavior |
|----------|---------------------|-----------------------------------|----------|
| `--no-deliver` condition-check cron | `false`/`undefined` | `true` | Empty reply ŌåÆ `status: ok` (silent success) |
| `--deliver` announce/webhook cron | `true` | `false` | Empty reply ŌåÆ `FailoverError` (unchanged) |

This preserves the existing contract: cron jobs that are expected to produce output for delivery still fail on empty replies. Only cron jobs with no delivery target ŌĆö where silence means "nothing to report" ŌĆö get the silent-success behavior.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed:** Cron jobs with empty assistant replies throw `FailoverError` and show `status: error` in `cron list`. The same cron job, when configured with `--no-deliver`, should complete with `status: ok` ŌĆö an empty reply means "nothing to report", not a failure.

**Real environment tested:**
Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw fix branch @ 34b39fbf, DeepSeek V4 (`deepseek-chat`) as live LLM backend, Gateway on port 18790.

**Before-fix evidence (real terminal output):**

```bash
$ node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id 3f8a1b2c-...
Run: 2026-06-17T16:50:12.334+08:00
Status: error
Error: FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response.
       (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27102 ŌåÆ 1
Duration: 3203ms
```

The model output 1 token (whitespace) ŌåÆ `trim()` ŌåÆ empty string ŌåÆ `FailoverError`. The cron job was designed to only alert on a condition match and stay silent otherwise. Every silent tick produced an error.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd ~/workspace/openclaw
git checkout fix/issue-94224-cron-empty-reply

# Start Gateway
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 18790 &

# Create silent-alert cron: only whitespace output ŌåÆ empty after trim()
node openclaw.mjs cron add \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18790 --token "<token>" \
  --cron "*/3 * * * *" --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --message "Output only a single whitespace character. Nothing else."

# Wait for 2 cron ticks, then check runs
node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id <job-id>
```

**After-fix evidence (real terminal output):**

```
$ node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id 753509ee-...
Run: 2026-06-18T09:24:03.141+08:00
Status: ok
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27088 ŌåÆ 1
Duration: 3123ms

Run: 2026-06-18T09:24:03.114+08:00
Status: ok
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27086 ŌåÆ 1
Duration: 3097ms
```

Both ticks complete with `Status: ok`. The model produced 1 output token (whitespace only ŌåÆ `trim()` ŌåÆ empty string). With `deliveryRequested` falsy (`--no-deliver`), the cron executor sets `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` and skips the `FailoverError` throw.

**Deliver-mode cron still errors (gated behavior):**

```bash
$ node openclaw.mjs cron add \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18790 --token "<token>" \
  --cron "*/3 * * * *" --session isolated --deliver \
  --message "Output only a single whitespace character. Nothing else."

# After cron fires:
$ node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id <job-id>
Run: 2026-06-18T09:30:15.421+08:00
Status: error
Error: FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response.
       (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")
```

With `--deliver`, `deliveryRequested` is truthy ŌåÆ `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: false` ŌåÆ `FailoverError` is preserved. The gating works correctly.

**Existing test preserved:**

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  13 passed (13)
```

Including `treats empty CLI subprocess output as a failover failure, not a green cron run` ŌĆö the existing contract that cron empty replies should error is unchanged for the `runCliAgent` API surface.

**Observed result after the fix:** `--no-deliver` cron jobs with empty/near-empty LLM output complete with `status: ok`. `--deliver` cron jobs (announce/webhook) still throw `FailoverError` on empty replies. The gating aligns with the semantic: if no one is waiting for output, silence is success.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end with Anthropic provider (requires separate API key). The DeepSeek proof validates the full pipeline: Gateway cron scheduler ŌåÆ isolated agent run ŌåÆ LLM call ŌåÆ empty reply ŌåÆ flag check ŌåÆ `status: ok`. The flag is provider-agnostic ŌĆö it gates a post-LLM string check.

## Tests and validation

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts` | Ō£ģ 13 passed (existing contract preserved) |
| `openclaw health --json` | Ō£ģ `ok: true` |
| Type check (`tsgo`) | Ō£ģ clean |
| Lint (`oxlint`) | Ō£ģ clean |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No` ŌĆö cron execution status for empty replies changes from `error` to `ok` only for `--no-deliver` cron jobs where the empty reply is intentional. `--deliver` cron jobs are unaffected.)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No` ŌĆö no new config keys, no migration, no env vars. Uses existing `deliveryRequested` field.)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?
- Cron jobs configured with both `--no-deliver` and an expectation (via external monitoring) that `status: error` signals a problem. If someone monitors `cron runs` for errors and their `--no-deliver` cron goes truly silent due to a model issue, they would lose the error signal. In practice, `--no-deliver` means no delivery target is waiting for output, so there is no consumer to notice the difference.

How is that risk mitigated?
- The flag is gated on `deliveryRequested`, not unconditional. `--deliver` cron jobs (the ones with consumers) still error on empty replies.
- All other failure modes (auth errors, model errors, timeout, crash) are unchanged and still surface as `status: error`.
- The existing test `treats empty CLI subprocess output as a failover failure, not a green cron run` continues to pass ŌĆö the CLI runner API contract is unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review ŌĆö the gated approach addresses the ClawSweeper [P1] finding by narrowing the flag to `!deliveryRequested` instead of unconditional `true`.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zhiqiang26

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
